skill.name: lead-magnets
skill.description: Plan lead magnets for email capture, lead generation, content upgrades, gated resources, templates, quizzes, webinars, and related distribution workflows.
skill.summary: Create practical lead magnet strategies that connect audience needs, formats, gating, delivery, promotion, and measurement.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: The static findings are false positives caused by marketing terms, Markdown inline code, and a vendor name containing Beacon. No executable code, shell invocation, data exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keywords
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts match ordinary marketing and format-guide text, such as ebook, checklist, template, and cost descriptions. These lines do not define cryptographic algorithms, secrets, encryption routines, or executable behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are content planning guidance, tables, and examples. There is no cryptographic code or instruction to use weak algorithms.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Cobalt Strike Keyword
low_findings.description: The Cobalt Strike alert is triggered by the product name Beacon.by in a list of design tools. It is not a command-and-control reference, payload instruction, or malware configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious token appears only as a legitimate marketing tool name in a tools list. No Cobalt Strike behavior or offensive security workflow is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Terms
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts occur in marketing benchmark and channel-planning text. The lines discuss lead timing, paid promotion, and distribution planning, not host discovery or system inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is marketing strategy and performance planning. There are no system commands, discovery steps, or instructions to inspect a user's environment.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Shell Backtick Execution
low_findings.description: The backtick alerts on SKILL.md line 15 are Markdown inline code around documentation paths. The skill asks the assistant to read product marketing context if it exists, but it does not execute shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks enclose file paths in prose, not Ruby or shell code. No command substitution, interpreter call, or user-controlled command string exists.

content.value_statement: Many teams offer generic downloads that collect emails but attract weak leads. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan focused lead magnets with clear format, gating, delivery, promotion, and measurement.
actual_capabilities: Recommends lead magnet formats based on buyer stage, audience needs, and available assets.
Outlines content scope for checklists, ebooks, templates, swipe files, mini-courses, quizzes, and webinars.
Plans gating approaches, form fields, landing page sections, delivery methods, and thank you page next steps.
Maps promotion channels across blog CTAs, popups, social media, paid promotion, and partner campaigns.
Defines measurement plans with conversion, lead quality, email engagement, and A/B testing ideas.
Uses benchmark references for expected conversion ranges, costs, timelines, and quality signals.
limitations: It plans strategy but does not write full lead magnet copy or design final assets.
It does not implement forms, popups, landing pages, quizzes, or tracking code.
Benchmarks are general guidance and require validation against the user's own traffic and audience.
It depends on accurate business, audience, product, and existing content context from the user.

use_cases.title: Create a First Lead Magnet
use_cases.description: Choose a practical offer, outline the content, and define a simple capture and delivery plan.

use_cases.title: Improve Existing Email Capture
use_cases.description: Diagnose weak opt-in offers and plan more specific upgrades for high-intent pages.

use_cases.title: Build Campaign Assets
use_cases.description: Turn blog posts, webinars, templates, or internal processes into lead generation offers.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Lead Magnet
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with basic business context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a lead magnet for my business. We sell [product], our audience is [audience], and our main goal is [goal].

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Content Upgrade
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a blog or resource page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a content upgrade plan for this page: [page topic or URL summary]. Recommend the offer, format, gate, and call to action.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize an Existing Offer
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with current performance data.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this lead magnet concept: [concept]. Current conversion rate is [rate], traffic source is [source], and lead quality is [quality signal].

prompt_templates.title: Design a Full Campaign
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for strategy, promotion, and measurement.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a complete lead magnet strategy for [audience] at the [buyer stage] stage. Include format, outline, gating, delivery, distribution, KPIs, and A/B tests.

output_examples.input: We sell analytics software to B2B marketers and need a top-funnel offer.
output_examples.output: A recommended offer such as a marketing measurement checklist, with buyer stage, outline, landing page plan, email delivery method, and success metrics.

output_examples.input: Our generic ebook gets downloads but few demo requests.
output_examples.output: A diagnosis of offer mismatch, plus a more specific template or assessment that creates a clearer path to the product.

output_examples.input: We have several strong blog posts and want more email subscribers.
output_examples.output: A list of content upgrade opportunities matched to posts, with suggested CTAs, gate levels, and first tests to run.

best_practices: Start with one specific audience problem and one clear buyer stage.
Ask for the minimum form fields needed to deliver and qualify the lead.
Measure lead quality after delivery, not only download volume.
anti_patterns: Do not create a broad ebook when a checklist or template would solve the problem faster.
Do not gate weak content that has no immediate value for the audience.
Do not promote the same generic offer on every page without matching page intent.

faq.question: Can this skill write the final lead magnet?
faq.answer: It focuses on planning and structure. Use a copywriting workflow for full drafts and final copy.

faq.question: Which formats does it support?
faq.answer: It supports checklists, cheat sheets, templates, swipe files, ebooks, mini-courses, quizzes, webinars, and resource libraries.

faq.question: Can it help with landing pages?
faq.answer: It can plan landing page sections, form fields, previews, social proof, and thank you page next steps.

faq.question: Does it support paid promotion planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It can include paid channels in the distribution plan, but it does not build ad campaigns.

faq.question: Can it use existing content?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks about current posts, guides, data, tools, and internal templates that can become offers.

faq.question: How should success be measured?
faq.answer: Track conversion rate, cost per lead, email engagement, lead quality, and progression to trial, demo, or purchase.
